title: B1 Listening Part 1 - Test 29
instructions: For each question, listen to the short recording and choose the correct picture: A, B or C.

questions.context: You hear two students talking about what to take on a forest walk.
questions.audio_script: Girl: Are you ready for the forest walk tomorrow? I was going to pack my camera, because there may be good views. || Boy: I saw the latest message from school. The main problem is the woods are damp. || Girl: Should I take my sandals? || Boy: Not really. [1]The teacher said everyone should bring insect spray[/1], because mosquitoes are bad this week. || Girl: Thanks. I’ll put that in my bag now, before I forget again. My bag is by the door already. || Girl: Let's check the plan once more before we leave, just to be sure.

dialogue.speaker: girl
dialogue.text: Are you ready for the forest walk tomorrow? I was going to pack my camera, because there may be good views.

dialogue.speaker: boy
dialogue.text: I saw the latest message from school. The main problem is the woods are damp.

dialogue.speaker: girl
dialogue.text: Should I take my sandals?

dialogue.speaker: boy
dialogue.text: Not really. The teacher said everyone should bring insect spray, because mosquitoes are bad this week.

dialogue.speaker: girl
dialogue.text: Thanks. I’ll put that in my bag now, before I forget again. My bag is by the door already.

dialogue.speaker: girl
dialogue.text: Let's check the plan once more before we leave, just to be sure.

questions.context: You hear a teacher giving information about the eco club.
questions.audio_script: Woman: Before you go, remember that the eco club is not in its usual place today. Some students thought it was in the science lab, but an exam is taking place there. Others may wait near the garden, although the grass is too wet. [1]Please go straight to the geography room at half past three[/1]. If your friend is absent this morning, send them a quick message so they do not arrive late. The teacher will start as soon as everyone is there.

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: Before you go, remember that the eco club is not in its usual place today. Some students thought it was in the science lab, but an exam is taking place there. Others may wait near the garden, although the grass is too wet. Please go straight to the geography room at half past three. If your friend is absent this morning, send them a quick message so they do not arrive late. The teacher will start as soon as everyone is there.

A.imagePrompt: Simple Cambridge exam illustration style, students in a science lab, no text or labels, flat educational drawing, black and white line art, minimal details, clean white background.

B.imagePrompt: Simple Cambridge exam illustration style, students looking at maps in a geography room, no text or labels, flat educational drawing, black and white line art, minimal details, clean white background.

C.imagePrompt: Simple Cambridge exam illustration style, students in a school garden, no text or labels, flat educational drawing, black and white line art, minimal details, clean white background.

questions.explanation: The teacher says to go straight to the geography room.

questions.context: You hear an announcement for passengers travelling to Cardiff.
questions.audio_script: Speaker: Good afternoon. This is a message for passengers travelling to Cardiff. You may have seen the earlier notice asking you to wait near the door three, but that information has changed. [1]Please go to the glass doors now[/1]. Do not use the back entrance, because the back entrance is locked. Staff will be nearby if you need help with luggage or directions. Please move carefully and keep your tickets ready for checking. Thank you for your patience today. || Speaker: Let's check the plan once more before we leave, just to be sure.

dialogue.speaker: speaker
dialogue.text: Good afternoon. This is a message for passengers travelling to Cardiff. You may have seen the earlier notice asking you to wait near the door three, but that information has changed. Please go to the glass doors now. Do not use the back entrance, because the back entrance is locked. Staff will be nearby if you need help with luggage or directions. Please move carefully and keep your tickets ready for checking. Thank you for your patience today.

dialogue.speaker: speaker
dialogue.text: Let's check the plan once more before we leave, just to be sure.

questions.question: Where should passengers go now?

A.imagePrompt: Simple Cambridge exam illustration style, people standing near a locked back entrance, no text or labels, flat educational drawing, black and white line art, minimal details, clean white background.

B.imagePrompt: Simple Cambridge exam illustration style, people entering through glass doors, no text or labels, flat educational drawing, black and white line art, minimal details, clean white background.

C.imagePrompt: Simple Cambridge exam illustration style, people sitting in a waiting room, no text or labels, flat educational drawing, black and white line art, minimal details, clean white background.

questions.explanation: The announcement says passengers should go to the glass doors now.

questions.context: You hear two people talking about a broken radio.
questions.audio_script: Woman: Did anyone fix your radio? || Man: Yes, but it wasn’t the problem I expected. I thought it was the aerial, because it looked odd yesterday. || Woman: Was it the speaker then? || Man: I wondered that too, because the sound kept cutting out. Then someone checked it carefully and [1]the radio worked when we used a different plug[/1]. || Woman: So it was quite simple. || Man: Yes, luckily. I need it tonight, so I’m glad it is working again. || Man: Let's check the plan once more before we leave, just to be sure.

dialogue.text: Did anyone fix your radio?

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: Yes, but it wasn’t the problem I expected. I thought it was the aerial, because it looked odd yesterday.

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: Was it the speaker then?

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: I wondered that too, because the sound kept cutting out. Then someone checked it carefully and the radio worked when we used a different plug.

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: So it was quite simple.

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: Yes, luckily. I need it tonight, so I’m glad it is working again.

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: Let's check the plan once more before we leave, just to be sure.

questions.explanation: The speaker says the radio worked when we used a different plug, so option B is correct.

questions.context: You hear two people choosing food at a hotel breakfast.
questions.audio_script: Girl: Have you chosen what to eat at the hotel breakfast? The queue is moving quickly. || Boy: I nearly chose the fried eggs, but eggs take too long. || Girl: What about the cereal? It looks popular today. || Boy: I thought about it, but cereal has nuts. [1]I’m going to have the toast[/1]. It should be enough before our journey. || Girl: Good choice. I’ll order mine after you, and then we can find a table near the window. || Girl: Let's check the plan once more before we leave, just to be sure.

questions.context: You hear two friends making plans after school.
questions.audio_script: Man: Are we still going to the zoo after school? || Woman: I don't think so. The tickets cost more than we expected. || Man: We could try the library instead. || Woman: Maybe, but we need to be outside today. My grandad has a family pass, so [1]we're going to on a boat trip this afternoon instead[/1]. || Man: That sounds fine. Shall we meet outside the bus station? || Woman: Yes, at four. Bring your student card, because they sometimes ask for it at the desk. || Woman: Let's check the plan once more before we leave, just to be sure.

questions.context: You hear two people talking about a present for my cousin.
questions.audio_script: Boy: I still need a present for my cousin. I thought about buying a football shirt, but that didn't seem special. || Girl: What about a skateboard? You mentioned that last week. || Boy: I checked, but the shop only had expensive ones. In the end, [1]I bought a science kit because she loves doing experiments[/1]. || Girl: That sounds perfect. Useful presents are often better than decorations. || Boy: I hope so. I’ll wrap it tonight after dinner, and take it over tomorrow. || Boy: Let's check the plan once more before we leave, just to be sure.
